description: secretx-k8s/src/lib.rs:80-91. map_kube_error routes kube::Error::Api(s) where s.is_forbidden() to SecretError::Unavailable. 403 Forbidden is a permanent RBAC denial — retrying will not resolve it. Mapping it to Unavailable causes callers (including secretx-cache and any retry layer) to loop on permission errors. Conversely, all other kube::Error variants — HyperError (network), Service (tower middleware), RustlsTls/OpensslTls (transport), Auth (token refresh), ReadEvents (io), Api(s) with code in [429, 500..=599] — are mapped to Backend (permanent), so genuinely transient failures are not retried. Compare with secretx-aws-sm/src/lib.rs:91-119 which correctly distinguishes ResourceNotFound, throttling/internal-service (Unavailable), and everything else (Backend). Trigger: any RBAC-denied caller will infinite-loop in a retry layer; any caller during a kube apiserver rolling restart (503) will give up immediately. Fix: invert the mapping — forbidden->Backend, network/transport/Service/Hyper/5xx/429->Unavailable, Api with is_not_found()->NotFound, everything else->Backend.
title: map_kube_error misclassifies forbidden as transient and transient errors as permanent
